title: The Role of Libraries in Modern Society
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Libraries have long been essential centres of (0) information and learning for communities worldwide. Despite the rise of the internet, libraries remain (17) irreplaceable spaces where people of all ages can access a wide range of resources. They provide a quiet (18) environment for study and offer (19) valuable support to those who lack internet access at home.||Modern libraries also host (20) educational programmes, workshops, and cultural events that bring communities together. Librarians are (21) highly trained professionals who help users find and evaluate (22) reliable sources of information. Many libraries now offer digital services, demonstrating their (23) remarkable ability to adapt. Far from becoming (24) obsolete, libraries continue to play a vital role in promoting lifelong learning.

example.text: Libraries have long been essential centres of (0) INFORMATION and learning for communities worldwide. (INFORM)

questions.explanation: The adjective 'irreplaceable' uses the prefix 'ir-' and suffix '-able' added to 'replace'.
The noun 'environment' is formed from 'environ' by adding the suffix '-ment'.
The adjective 'valuable' is formed from 'value' by replacing '-e' with '-able'.
The adjective 'educational' is formed from 'educate' by replacing '-ate' with '-ational'.
The adverb 'highly' is formed from the adjective 'high' by adding the suffix '-ly'.
The adjective 'reliable' is formed from 'rely' by changing '-y' to '-iable'.
The adjective 'remarkable' is formed from 'remark' by adding the suffix '-able'.
The adjective 'obsolete' means 'no longer used'; it is the root form from which 'obsolescence' derives.
